key_insight: LiL-Q turns nonlinear PDE solving into a sequence of convex linear least-squares problems by combining Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization with physics-informed trial spaces whose trainable parameters enter linearly.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more stable and scalable alternative to standard PINN training, especially for nonlinear PDEs where gradient-based optimization is slow, sensitive, or nonconvex.
method: Quasilinearize the nonlinear PDE into linear subproblems.
Discretize each subproblem with a LiL trial space and solve by direct QR least-squares.
Use PINN-style physics constraints but avoid iterative gradient descent on network weights.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on seven benchmarks, including Bratu, Burgers, Buckley-Leverett, elasticity, Navier-Stokes in 2D/3D, and heterogeneous Darcy flow, and it often converged in single-digit outer iterations with machine-precision recovery when the exact solution was representable.
watch_item: Performance depends on the trial-space approximation quality and the stated local convergence condition, so accuracy may stall at the best-approximation residual and the method may not be globally robust without a good initial regime.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
